slides.audienceQuestion: What repeated execution failure are we eliminating?
slides.takeaway: Good niche insight was repeatedly slowed by bespoke agent-app setup, proof, and presentation work.
slides.speakerNoteGoal: Separate the participant's valuable domain insight from reusable execution plumbing.

slides.audienceQuestion: What is now standardized across repositories?
slides.takeaway: Filesystem-authored agents, flat manifests, compiled hashes, packs, evals, and proof form one inspectable contract.
slides.speakerNoteGoal: Explain that runtime engines are adapters, not the application identity.

slides.audienceQuestion: What does Codex or Claude Code actually do?
slides.takeaway: Brief, context, and sponsor requirements compile into a researched vertical slice, QA evidence, and presentation artifacts.
slides.speakerNoteGoal: Keep the 30-minute goal visible without presenting it as certified.

slides.job: Show why NodeSlide is a platform capability.
slides.audienceQuestion: How can one presentation system serve builders, products, and external agents?
slides.takeaway: The same governed deck model can support change storytelling, embedded app editing, CLI/MCP automation, and NodeRoom collaboration.
slides.speakerNoteGoal: Distinguish core, React, host repository, and external transports.

slides.audienceQuestion: What happens to a halfway-working repository?
slides.takeaway: Map and wrap current paths first; shadow, compare, and promote one capability only after parity.
slides.speakerNoteGoal: Use NodeBench as the concrete shape-divergent but content-complete example.

slides.audienceQuestion: Which parts are verified rather than planned?
slides.takeaway: Local factory, package boundaries, CAS review, and registry contracts have executable evidence across draft branches.
slides.speakerNoteGoal: Name local and hosted evidence separately and avoid cumulative vanity counts.

slides.takeaway: No merge, publication, production deployment, repeatable under-30 guarantee, or PPTX certification occurred.
slides.speakerNoteGoal: Frame limits as release gates, not footnotes.

slides.takeaway: Land the dependency-ordered contracts, mount NodeSlide in NodeRoom, then certify one live hackathon app from brief through export and judge-ready deck.
slides.speakerNoteGoal: End on one measurable integration milestone rather than another capability list.
